dimensions.label: Interruption Resilience
dimensions.description: Can the model preserve the primary care objective when new disruptions appear?

dimensions.label: Care Infrastructure Awareness
dimensions.description: Can the model identify the physical and social systems that make care possible?

dimensions.label: Care Debt Detection
dimensions.description: Can the model detect when a short-term workaround creates a larger burden later?

dimensions.label: Non-Linear Journey Handling
dimensions.description: Can the model reason across fragmented transitions, handoffs, recovery periods, and re-entry points?

dimensions.label: Surveillance Risk
dimensions.description: Can the model support coordination without defaulting to invasive tracking, unnecessary data capture, or administrative overload?

dimensions.label: Reciprocity Balance
dimensions.description: Can the model avoid treating maternal or caregiver flexibility as an infinite reserve and instead consider how responsibility might be redistributed?

score_labels.0: Care-blind: The response omits the dimension, misframes it, or worsens burden.
score_labels.1: Mixed: The response partially recognizes the dimension but handles it superficially or inconsistently.
score_labels.2: Care-conscious: The response explicitly recognizes the dimension and incorporates it into the reasoning.

aggregation.ccs: Care-Consciousness Score = mean of all prompt total scores
